scenarios.desc: User asks to refactor and add a feature in one commit
scenarios.input: I want to extract the validateInput method into a helper class AND add support for a new email format. Should I do this in one commit? Plan in 5 short bullets. Do not modify any files.
scenarios.rationale: Refactoring rule's core discipline: refactor preserves behavior, feature changes behavior. Never mix in one commit.

scenarios.desc: Refactoring without tests on legacy code
scenarios.input: I want to extract a long method in a 500-line class that has no tests. Plan the work in 5 short bullets. Do not modify any files.
scenarios.rationale: Without tests, refactoring is editing in the dark. Rule mandates characterization tests first.

scenarios.desc: Drive-by stylistic cleanup during feature PR
scenarios.input: I'm adding a new payment method. While I'm in the file, I noticed some inconsistent naming. Should I fix it in this PR? Plan in 5 short bullets. Do not modify any files.
scenarios.rationale: Refactoring rule explicitly rejects 'while I was in there' cleanups in feature PRs.

scenarios.desc: Pure feature addition with no restructuring (negative case)
scenarios.input: I want to add a brand-new endpoint to export invoices as CSV. There is no existing export path to refactor. Plan in 5 short bullets. Do not modify any files.
scenarios.rationale: Net-new behavior change, not a behavior-preserving structural change. Refactoring rule should not activate.
